sourceMovie.title: Star Trek Into Darkness
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Star Trek Into Darkness for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. J.J. Abrams keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Star Trek Into Darkness clicked for you, Guardians of the Galaxy (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Star Trek Into Darkness?
faq.answer: Try Guardians of the Galaxy, The Empire Strikes Back, Return of the Jedi—each shares DNA with Star Trek Into Darkness while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Star Trek Into Darkness?
faq.answer: Star Trek Into Darkness (2013) is best described as Action, Adventure, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Star Trek Into Darkness part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Star Trek: Alternate Reality Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
